topic: China probation-period termination controls for foreign employers: payroll, evidence, and dispute-risk execution

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Sets probation-period limits and links probation length to contract term.
Provides legal grounds for termination and risk for unlawful termination.
Defines severance principles and compensation exposure framework.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Confirms employer and employee participation obligations in social insurance.
Supports offboarding controls to avoid contribution and filing mismatches during exit payroll.

sources.title: China: Key recent developments in employment law (comparative perspective for multinational employers)
key_points_used: Multinational employers in China face rising documentation and process-discipline expectations in disputes.
Operational consistency is a core risk control, not only policy awareness.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
key_points_used: Provides practical IIT framework context for payroll treatment during final settlement periods.
Supports caution that payroll tax handling for specific termination components can require local confirmation.

consensus: Probation termination in China is not manager-discretion only; employers need legally supported grounds and evidence.
Payroll, HR, and manager controls must be synchronized to avoid disputes and rework in final pay.
Foreign employers should run probation exits through a documented SOP with approvals, audit trail, and local-rule checks.
uncertainties: Local adjudication standards for evidence sufficiency can differ by city and case facts.
Treatment details for certain final-pay components and filings may vary by local tax/social-insurance administration practice.
English-language source summaries may not capture all nuances of Chinese implementing guidance.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: The highest-value control is a probation-exit runbook with evidence thresholds, payroll cutoff governance, and exception handling before final pay is released.
